{"type":"text","timestamp":1775687261690,"sessionID":"ses_290ce3d60ffeXnDrliYe6EimIp","part":{"id":"prt_d6f32f8c7001lj9kXsilEP2GfT","messageID":"msg_d6f32df38001Cfe83ucaOn74JA","sessionID":"ses_290ce3d60ffeXnDrliYe6EimIp","type":"text","text":"Now I will produce the complete architecture document. Given the scope, I will write it as a comprehensive artifact. --- # Architecture Decision Record: Payment Processing Platform Microservices Architecture **ADR Number:** ADR-2026-001 **Date:** 2026-04-08 **Status:** ACCEPTED **Deciders:** Architect Lead, Engineering Leadership **Context:** This architecture must replace a monolithic payment service hitting scaling limits at 1,200 TPS, targeting 5,000 TPS for Q3 premium tier launch. --- ## 1. Context and Problem Statement **Problem:** The current monolithic payment service cannot scale beyond 1,200 TPS. Business requirements mandate 5,000 TPS by Q3 with p99 latency under 200ms. The monolith prevents parallel implementation across teams and creates unacceptable blast radius for failures. **Scope:** Design a decomposed microservices architecture for a payment processing platform that handles: - Credit card transactions (authorization, capture, void) - Refunds (full and partial) - Subscription billing (recurring charges, plan management) **Constraints:** - PCI-DSS Level 1 compliance required - AWS us-east-1 region with multi-AZ deployment - EKS 1.28 Kubernetes cluster - RDS PostgreSQL 15.4 for persistent state - Kafka for asynchronous inter-service communication **Assumptions (labeled for transparency):** - A1: Business has existing relationships with card networks (Visa, Mastercard) and issuing banks (assumed via payment gateway integration) - A2: Merchant onboarding is out of scope for Phase 1 - A3: Multi-currency support is Phase 2 --- ## 2. Decision Drivers 1. **Scalability**: Horizontal scaling required to achieve 5,000 TPS 2. **PCI-DSS Compliance**: Cardholder data must be isolated in a minimal PCI-DSS scope 3. **Latency**: p99 < 200ms for transaction authorization 4. **Resilience**: Circuit breakers, retry policies, bulkheads required 5. **Observability**: Distributed tracing across service boundaries 6. **Team Velocity**: Independent deployment and parallel development --- ## 3. Considered Options ### Option A: Pure Event-Driven (All Async via Kafka) **Pros:** - Maximum decoupling between services - Natural backpressure handling - High throughput potential **Cons:** - Eventual consistency complicates transaction workflows - Distributed sagas required for multi-step transactions (complexity) - Debugging and tracing harder without synchronous anchors **Assessment:** Rejected for core payment flow. Appropriate for non-critical async workflows (notifications, analytics). ### Option B: Hybrid (Sync REST + Async Kafka) **Pros:** - Synchronous anchors provide immediate consistency where needed - Async for non-blocking workflows (notifications, ledger updates) - Easier debugging with synchronous traces - Matches Stripe/Adyen/Square published patterns **Cons:** - More complex infrastructure (two protocols) - Requires careful boundary design to avoid distributed monolith **Assessment:** **SELECTED** - This is the pattern used by major payment processors. ### Option C: GraphQL Federation **Pros:** - Flexible client queries - Schema federation **Cons:** - N+1 query problems in payment domain - WebSocket subscriptions add complexity - Less mature tooling for high-throughput payment APIs **Assessment:** Rejected. REST is more appropriate for payment APIs with well-defined operations. --- ## 4. Decision Outcome **Chosen Pattern:** Hybrid Synchronous/Asynchronous Microservices Architecture - **Client-facing APIs**: Synchronous REST (OpenAPI 3.1) for immediate response - **Inter-service communication**: Asynchronous Kafka for event-driven workflows - **PCI-DSS Scope Isolation**: Tokenization Service as the boundary - card data never leaves this service in raw form --- ## 5. Service Boundary Diagram ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Client-Facing Zone (Non-PCI Scope)" GW[API Gateway
Rate Limiting, Auth] PAY[Payment Service] REF[Refund Service] SUB[Subscription Service] NOT[Notification Service] end subgraph "PCI-DSS Scope Boundary (Cardholder Data Environment)" TOK[Tokenization Service
Card Vault] RISK[Risk/Fraud Service] end subgraph "Core Domain (Non-PCI Scope)" LED[Ledger Service
Double-Entry Bookkeeping] SET[settlement Service] end subgraph "Shared Infrastructure" KAFKA[Kafka
Event Bus] PG[(RDS PostgreSQL
Per-Service Schemas)] end GW -->|REST| PAY GW -->|REST| REF GW -->|REST| SUB PAY -->|async events| KAFKA REF -->|async events| KAFKA SUB -->|async events| KAFKA KAFKA -->|consume| LED KAFKA -->|consume| NOT KAFKA -->|consume| RISK PAY -->|sync| TOK REF -->|sync| TOK PAY -->|sync| RISK LED -->|write| PG PAY -->|write| PG REF -->|write| PG SUB -->|write| PG TOK -->|write| PG RISK -->|write| PG SET -->|write| PG ``` **PCI-DSS Scope Boundary Rationale:** - **In Scope (CDE)**: Tokenization Service, Risk/Fraud Service (processes raw card data) - **Out of Scope**: All other services operate on tokens only --- ## 6. API Contracts ### 6.1 API Gateway Service **Base Path:** `/api/v1` **Authentication:** OAuth 2.0 / JWT **Rate Limiting:** 5,000 TPS aggregate, per-client limits enforced ```yaml openapi: 3.1.0 info: title: Payment Platform API Gateway version: 1.0.0 description: Client-facing API gateway for payment processing paths: /payments: post: operationId: createPayment summary: Create a new payment transaction tags: [Payments] requestBody: required: true content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/PaymentRequest' responses: '201': description: Payment created successfully headers: X-Request-Id: schema: type: string description: Unique request identifier for tracing X-RateLimit-Remaining: schema: type: integer X-RateLimit-Reset: schema: type: integer format: int64 content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/PaymentResponse' '400': $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest' '401': $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' '422': $ref: '#/components/responses/UnprocessableEntity' '429': $ref: '#/components/responses/RateLimited' '500': $ref: '#/components/responses/InternalError' get: operationId: getPayments summary: List payments with pagination parameters: - $ref: '#/components/parameters/PageToken' - $ref: '#/components/parameters/PageSize' - name: status in: query schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/PaymentStatus' responses: '200': description: List of payments content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/PaymentListResponse' /payments/{paymentId}: get: operationId: getPayment summary: Get payment by ID parameters: - name: paymentId in: path required: true schema: type: string format: uuid responses: '200': description: Payment details content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/PaymentResponse' '404': $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' /payments/{paymentId}/void: post: operationId: voidPayment summary: Void an authorized payment parameters: - name: paymentId in: path required: true schema: type: string format: uuid requestBody: required: true content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/VoidRequest' responses: '200': description: Payment voided content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/PaymentResponse' /refunds: post: operationId: createRefund summary: Create a refund tags: [Refunds] requestBody: required: true content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/RefundRequest' responses: '201': description: Refund created content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/RefundResponse' /subscriptions: post: operationId: createSubscription summary: Create a subscription tags: [Subscriptions] requestBody: required: true content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/SubscriptionRequest' responses: '201': description: Subscription created content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/SubscriptionResponse' get: operationId: getSubscriptions summary: List subscriptions parameters: - $ref: '#/components/parameters/PageToken' - $ref: '#/components/parameters/PageSize' responses: '200': description: List of subscriptions content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/SubscriptionListResponse' /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}: get: operationId: getSubscription summary: Get subscription details parameters: - name: subscriptionId in: path required: true schema: type: string format: uuid responses: '200': description: Subscription details content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/SubscriptionResponse' patch: operationId: updateSubscription summary: Update subscription plan or status parameters: - name: subscriptionId in: path required: true schema: type: string format: uuid requestBody: required: true content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/SubscriptionUpdateRequest' responses: '200': description: Subscription updated content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/SubscriptionResponse' delete: operationId: cancelSubscription summary: Cancel a subscription parameters: - name: subscriptionId in: path required: true schema: type: string format: uuid responses: '200': description: Subscription cancelled /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/billing-cycles: get: operationId: getBillingCycles summary: Get billing history for subscription parameters: - name: subscriptionId in: path required: true schema: type: string format: uuid - $ref: '#/components/parameters/PageToken' - $ref: '#/components/parameters/PageSize' responses: '200': description: Billing cycles content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/BillingCycleListResponse' components: parameters: PageToken: name: page_token in: query schema: type: string description: Cursor for pagination PageSize: name: page_size in: query schema: type: integer minimum: 1 maximum: 100 default: 20 schemas: PaymentRequest: type: object required: [amount, currency, paymentMethodId] properties: amount: type: integer description: Amount in smallest currency unit (cents) minimum: 50 example: 4999 currency: type: string minLength: 3 maxLength: 3 example: USD paymentMethodId: type: string description: Tokenized payment method ID (from client-side tokenization) example: pm_1234abcd idempotencyKey: type: string description: Client-provided key for idempotent requests example: cust_123_sub_456_20260408 metadata: type: object additionalProperties: type: string PaymentResponse: type: object properties: id: type: string format: uuid status: $ref: '#/components/schemas/PaymentStatus' amount: type: integer currency: type: string paymentMethodId: type: string createdAt: type: string format: date-time updatedAt: type: string format: date-time PaymentStatus: type: string enum: [pending, authorized, captured, voided, failed] PaymentListResponse: type: object properties: data: type: array items: $ref: '#/components/schemas/PaymentResponse' nextPageToken: type: string hasMore: type: boolean VoidRequest: type: object properties: reason: type: string maxLength: 500 RefundRequest: type: object required: [paymentId, amount] properties: paymentId: type: string format: uuid amount: type: integer description: Amount to refund in smallest currency unit minimum: 1 reason: type: string enum: [duplicate, fraudulent, customer_request] idempotencyKey: type: string RefundResponse: type: object properties: id: type: string format: uuid paymentId: type: string format: uuid amount: type: integer status: type: string enum: [pending, processed, failed] reason: type: string createdAt: type: string format: date-time SubscriptionRequest: type: object required: [customerId, planId, paymentMethodId] properties: customerId: type: string format: uuid planId: type: string paymentMethodId: type: string idempotencyKey: type: string trialDays: type: integer minimum: 0 maximum: 90 default: 0 SubscriptionResponse: type: object properties: id: type: string format: uuid customerId: type: string format: uuid planId: type: string status: type: string enum: [trialing, active, past_due, canceled, expired] currentPeriodStart: type: string format: date-time currentPeriodEnd: type: string format: date-time createdAt: type: string format: date-time SubscriptionUpdateRequest: type: object properties: planId: type: string defaultPaymentMethodId: type: string cancelAtPeriodEnd: type: boolean SubscriptionListResponse: type: object properties: data: type: array items: $ref: '#/components/schemas/SubscriptionResponse' nextPageToken: type: string hasMore: type: boolean BillingCycleListResponse: type: object properties: data: type: array items: $ref: '#/components/schemas/BillingCycle' nextPageToken: type: string BillingCycle: type: object properties: id: type: string format: uuid subscriptionId: type: string format: uuid periodStart: type: string format: date-time periodEnd: type: string format: date-time amount: type: integer status: type: string enum: [pending, paid, failed] paymentId: type: string format: uuid responses: BadRequest: description: Invalid request content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' Unauthorized: description: Authentication required content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' NotFound: description: Resource not found content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' UnprocessableEntity: description: Business rule violation content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' RateLimited: description: Rate limit exceeded headers: X-RateLimit-RetryAfter: schema: type: integer content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' InternalError: description: Internal server error content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' Error: type: object required: [code, message] properties: code: type: string description: Machine-readable error code example: payment_declined message: type: string description: Human-readable error message details: type: object additionalProperties: true requestId: type: string ``` ### 6.2 Payment Service **Service Name:** `payment-service` **Internal API Path:** `/internal/v1/payments` **Database Schema:** `payments` **Topics Consumed:** None **Topics Produced:** `payment.events` ```yaml openapi: 3.1.0 info: title: Payment Service version: 1.0.0 description: Internal payment processing service paths: /internal/v1/payments: post: operationId: processPayment summary: Process a payment authorization and capture requestBody: required: true content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/ProcessPaymentRequest' responses: '200': description: Payment processed content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/Payment' '422': description: Payment declined content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/PaymentDeclinedError' /internal/v1/payments/{paymentId}/authorize: post: operationId: authorizePayment summary: Authorize payment without capture parameters: - name: paymentId in: path required: true schema: type: string format: uuid responses: '200': description: Authorization successful content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/Payment' /internal/v1/payments/{paymentId}/capture: post: operationId: capturePayment summary: Capture a previously authorized payment parameters: - name: paymentId in: path required: true schema: type: string format: uuid requestBody: required: true content: application/json: schema: type: object properties: amount: type: integer description: Amount to capture (defaults to full authorized amount) responses: '200': description: Capture successful content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/Payment' components: schemas: ProcessPaymentRequest: type: object required: [tokenizedPaymentMethodId, amount, currency, merchantId, idempotencyKey] properties: tokenizedPaymentMethodId: type: string description: Token from Tokenization Service (not raw card data) amount: type: integer currency: type: string merchantId: type: string idempotencyKey: type: string fraudSessionId: type: string description: Session ID for fraud checking Payment: type: object properties: id: type: string format: uuid merchantId: type: string amount: type: integer currency: type: string status: type: string enum: [authorized, captured, voided, failed] networkReference: type: string description: Card network authorization code createdAt: type: string format: date-time PaymentDeclinedError: type: object properties: code: type: string example: insufficient_funds message: type: string declineCode: type: string enum: [insufficient_funds, card_declined, expired_card, processing_error] ``` ### 6.3 Tokenization Service (PCI-DSS CDE Boundary) **Service Name:** `tokenization-service` **Internal API Path:** `/internal/v1/tokens` **Database Schema:** `token_vault` (AES-256 encrypted) **PCI-DSS Scope:** FULL CDE - This is the only service that handles raw PAN ```yaml openapi: 3.1.0 info: title: Tokenization Service version: 1.0.0 description: PCI-DSS scoped tokenization and card vault service paths: /internal/v1/tokens: post: operationId: tokenizeCard summary: Create a token from card details (used by client SDK) security: - BearerAuth: [] requestBody: required: true content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/TokenizeRequest' responses: '201': description: Token created content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/TokenResponse' /internal/v1/tokens/{tokenId}: get: operationId: getTokenMetadata summary: Get token metadata (not raw PAN) security: - BearerAuth: [] parameters: - name: tokenId in: path required: true schema: type: string responses: '200': description: Token metadata content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/TokenMetadata' '404': description: Token not found delete: operationId: deleteToken summary: Delete a token security: - BearerAuth: [] parameters: - name: tokenId in: path required: true schema: type: string responses: '204': description: Token deleted /internal/v1/tokens/{tokenId}/reveal: post: operationId: revealToken summary: Reveal token for network processing (internal only) security: - ServiceAuth: [] parameters: - name: tokenId in: path required: true schema: type: string responses: '200': description: Raw PAN (never logged, never cached) content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/RevealResponse' components: schemas: TokenizeRequest: type: object required: [encryptedCardData] properties: encryptedCardData: type: string description: Client-side encrypted card data customerId: type: string format: uuid TokenResponse: type: object properties: tokenId: type: string example: pm_a1b2c3d4e5f6 fingerprint: type: string description: Unique fingerprint of the card expiresAt: type: string format: date-time TokenMetadata: type: object properties: tokenId: type: string last4: type: string brand: type: string enum: [visa, mastercard, amex, discover] expMonth: type: integer expYear: type: integer fingerprint: type: string createdAt: type: string format: date-time RevealResponse: type: object properties: pan: type: string description: Raw PAN - strict access control, no logging cvv: type: string ``` ### 6.4 Refund Service **Service Name:** `refund-service` **Internal API Path:** `/internal/v1/refunds` **Database Schema:** `refunds` **Topics Consumed:** `payment.events` (to update refund status) **Topics Produced:** `refund.events` ```yaml openapi: 3.1.0 info: title: Refund Service version: 1.0.0 paths: /internal/v1/refunds: post: operationId: processRefund summary: Process a refund requestBody: required: true content: application/json: schema: type: object required: [paymentId, amount, idempotencyKey] properties: paymentId: type: string format: uuid amount: type: integer reason: type: string enum: [duplicate, fraudulent, customer_request] idempotencyKey: type: string responses: '200': description: Refund processed content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/Refund' /internal/v1/refunds/{refundId}: get: operationId: getRefund parameters: - name: refundId in: path required: true schema: type: string format: uuid responses: '200': description: Refund details content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/Refund' components: schemas: Refund: type: object properties: id: type: string format: uuid paymentId: type: string format: uuid amount: type: integer status: type: string enum: [pending, processing, completed, failed] reason: type: string networkReference: type: string createdAt: type: string format: date-time completedAt: type: string format: date-time ``` ### 6.5 Subscription Service **Service Name:** `subscription-service` **Internal API Path:** `/internal/v1/subscriptions` **Database Schema:** `subscriptions`, `plans`, `billing_cycles` **Topics Consumed:** `subscription.commands`, `payment.events` **Topics Produced:** `subscription.events`, `subscription.commands` ```yaml openapi: 3.1.0 info: title: Subscription Service version: 1.0.0 paths: /internal/v1/subscriptions: post: operationId: createSubscription requestBody: required: true content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/CreateSubscriptionRequest' responses: '201': description: Subscription created content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/Subscription' /internal/v1/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}: patch: operationId: updateSubscription parameters: - name: subscriptionId in: path required: true schema: type: string format: uuid requestBody: required: true content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/UpdateSubscriptionRequest' responses: '200': description: Subscription updated delete: operationId: cancelSubscription parameters: - name: subscriptionId in: path required: true schema: type: string format: uuid responses: '200': description: Subscription canceled /internal/v1/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/retry: post: operationId: retryBilling summary: Retry failed billing payment parameters: - name: subscriptionId in: path required: true schema: type: string format: uuid responses: '200': description: Retry initiated components: schemas: CreateSubscriptionRequest: type: object required: [customerId, planId, paymentMethodToken] properties: customerId: type: string format: uuid planId: type: string paymentMethodToken: type: string trialDays: type: integer default: 0 idempotencyKey: type: string Subscription: type: object properties: id: type: string format: uuid customerId: type: string format: uuid planId: type: string status: type: string enum: [trialing, active, past_due, canceled, expired] currentPeriodStart: type: string format: date-time currentPeriodEnd: type: string format: date-time cancelAtPeriodEnd: type: boolean UpdateSubscriptionRequest: type: object properties: planId: type: string paymentMethodToken: type: string cancelAtPeriodEnd: type: boolean ``` ### 6.6 Ledger Service (Double-Entry Bookkeeping) **Service Name:** `ledger-service` **Database Schema:** `ledger_entries`, `accounts` **Topics Consumed:** `payment.events`, `refund.events`, `subscription.events` ```yaml openapi: 3.1.0 info: title: Ledger Service version: 1.0.0 paths: /internal/v1/ledger/entries: post: operationId: createLedgerEntry summary: Create a ledger entry (for internal use) requestBody: required: true content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/LedgerEntryRequest' responses: '201': description: Entry created get: operationId: getLedgerEntries summary: Query ledger entries parameters: - name: accountId in: query schema: type: string - name: from in: query schema: type: string format: date-time - name: to in: query schema: type: string format: date-time - $ref: '#/components/parameters/PageToken' - $ref: '#/components/parameters/PageSize' responses: '200': description: Ledger entries /internal/v1/ledger/balance: get: operationId: getBalance summary: Get account balance parameters: - name: accountId in: query required: true schema: type: string responses: '200': description: Balance content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/Balance' components: parameters: PageToken: name: page_token in: query schema: type: string PageSize: name: page_size in: query schema: type: integer default: 50 maximum: 200 schemas: LedgerEntryRequest: type: object required: [accountId, amount, currency, type, referenceId, referenceType] properties: accountId: type: string amount: type: integer description: Positive for debit, negative for credit currency: type: string type: type: string enum: [debit, credit] referenceId: type: string format: uuid referenceType: type: string enum: [payment, refund, subscription_charge, settlement] metadata: type: object LedgerEntry: type: object properties: id: type: string format: uuid accountId: type: string amount: type: integer currency: type: string type: type: string referenceId: type: string referenceType: type: string createdAt: type: string format: date-time Balance: type: object properties: accountId: type: string amount: type: integer currency: type: string asOf: type: string format: date-time ``` ### 6.7 Risk/Fraud Service **Service Name:** `risk-service` **Topics Consumed:** `payment.events`, `subscription.events` **Topics Produced:** `risk.evaluation.complete` ```yaml openapi: 3.1.0 info: title: Risk Service version: 1.0.0 paths: /internal/v1/risk/evaluate: post: operationId: evaluateRisk summary: Synchronous risk evaluation requestBody: required: true content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/RiskEvaluationRequest' responses: '200': description: Risk evaluation result content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/RiskEvaluation' components: schemas: RiskEvaluationRequest: type: object required: [paymentId, amount, currency, customerId, paymentMethodToken] properties: paymentId: type: string format: uuid amount: type: integer currency: type: string customerId: type: string format: uuid paymentMethodToken: type: string sessionId: type: string ipAddress: type: string userAgent: type: string RiskEvaluation: type: object properties: paymentId: type: string format: uuid score: type: number format: float minimum: 0 maximum: 1 decision: type: string enum: [approve, review, decline] reasons: type: array items: type: string rulesTriggered: type: array items: type: string evaluatedAt: type: string format: date-time ``` ### 6.8 Settlement Service **Service Name:** `settlement-service` **Database Schema:** `settlements`, `batches` **Topics Consumed:** `payment.events`, `refund.events` ```yaml openapi: 3.1.0 info: title: Settlement Service version: 1.0.0 paths: /internal/v1/settlements: get: operationId: getSettlements summary: List settlements parameters: - name: merchantId in: query schema: type: string - $ref: '#/components/parameters/PageToken' - $ref: '#/components/parameters/PageSize' responses: '200': description: Settlements /internal/v1/settlements/{settlementId}: get: operationId: getSettlement parameters: - name: settlementId in: path required: true schema: type: string format: uuid responses: '200': description: Settlement details components: parameters: PageToken: name: page_token in: query schema: type: string PageSize: name: page_size in: query schema: type: integer default: 20 schemas: Settlement: type: object properties: id: type: string format: uuid merchantId: type: string amount: type: integer currency: type: string status: type: string enum: [pending, processing, completed, failed] transactionCount: type: integer refundCount: type: integer netAmount: type: integer scheduledAt: type: string format: date-time completedAt: type: string format: date-time ``` --- ## 7. Infrastructure Sizing Table ### 8.1 Compute and Memory Sizing (Per Service) | Service | Replicas (Prod) | CPU (cores) | Memory (Gi) | JVM/Node Heap | Notes | |---------|-----------------|-------------|-------------|---------------|-------| | API Gateway | 3-10 (HPA) | 4 | 8 | N/A | Envoy-based, CPU-bound | | Payment Service | 3-20 (HPA) | 8 | 16 | 12Gi heap | Java/Spring Boot, high CPU | | Tokenization Service | 2-6 (HPA) | 4 | 8 | 6Gi heap | PCI-DSS isolated, encrypted storage | | Refund Service | 2-10 (HPA) | 2 | 4 | 3Gi heap | Light CPU, I/O bound | | Subscription Service | 2-10 (HPA) | 2 | 4 | 3Gi heap | Scheduled jobs, moderate | | Ledger Service | 2-6 (HPA) | 4 | 8 | 6Gi heap | Write-heavy, fsync latency critical | | Risk Service | 2-10 (HPA) | 8 | 16 | 12Gi heap | ML inference, high CPU/GPU | | Settlement Service | 2-4 (HPA) | 2 | 4 | 3Gi heap | Batch jobs, scheduled | | Notification Service | 2-6 (HPA) | 1 | 2 | 1.5Gi heap | Async, I/O bound | | Kafka (MSK) | 3 brokers | 8 | 32 | N/A | i3.4xlarge, 2TB NVMe | | Redis (ElastiCache) | 3 nodes | 4 | 15 | N/A | r6g.xlarge | ### 8.2 Database Sizing (RDS PostgreSQL 15.4) | Schema | Instance | vCPU | Memory | Storage | IOPS | Connection Pool | |--------|----------|------|--------|---------|------|----------------| | payments | db.r6g.4xlarge | 16 | 128Gi | 2TB SSD | 12000 | PgBouncer 200 | | refunds | db.r6g.2xlarge | 8 | 64Gi | 500GB SSD | 6000 | PgBouncer 100 | | subscriptions | db.r6g.2xlarge | 8 | 64Gi | 500GB SSD | 6000 | PgBouncer 100 | | ledger | db.r6g.4xlarge | 16 | 128Gi | 2TB SSD | 12000 | PgBouncer 200 | | token_vault | db.r6g.2xlarge | 8 | 64Gi | 200GB SSD | 6000 | PgBouncer 50 | | risk | db.r6g.2xlarge | 8 | 64Gi | 1TB SSD | 6000 | PgBouncer 100 | | settlements | db.r6g.xlarge | 4 | 32Gi | 500GB SSD | 3000 | PgBouncer 50 | ### 8.3 Network Bandwidth | Component | Inbound | Outbound | Notes | |-----------|---------|----------|-------| | API Gateway | 10 Gbps | 10 Gbps | Per node, NLB in front | | Payment Service | 5 Gbps | 5 Gbps | Card network calls | | Tokenization Service | 2 Gbps | 2 Gbps | Internal only | | Kafka (MSK) | 20 Gbps | 20 Gbps | 3 brokers total | ### 8.4 Kafka Topic Configuration | Topic | Partitions | Replication Factor | Retention | Throughput | |-------|------------|-------------------|----------|------------| | payment.events | 32 | 3 | 7 days | 5,000 msg/s | | refund.events | 16 | 3 | 7 days | 500 msg/s | | subscription.events | 16 | 3 | 7 days | 200 msg/s | | risk.evaluation.complete | 16 | 3 | 3 days | 5,000 msg/s | | notification.events | 8 | 3 | 3 days | 500 msg/s | ### 8.5 Target Capacity at 5,000 TPS **Assumption (A4):** Average payment message size ~2KB after compression. | Metric | Value | Notes | |--------|-------|-------| | Total TPS | 5,000 | Peak throughput target | | p99 Latency | < 200ms | End-to-end authorization | | p95 Latency | < 100ms | Target internal SLA | | Availability | 99.99% | Multi-AZ, no single point of failure | | Error Rate | < 0.1% | Including retries | | Kafka Throughput | ~10 MB/s | Payment events only | --- ## 9. Consequences and Risks ### 9.1 Positive Consequences 1. **Horizontal Scalability**: Each service scales independently, enabling 5,000+ TPS 2. **PCI-DSS Scope Reduction**: Card data isolated to Tokenization Service only 3. **Team Autonomy**: Services can be owned by separate teams with independent deploy cycles 4. **Fault Isolation**: Failure in Risk Service does not block Payment Service 5. **Technology Flexibility**: Each service can use optimal language/framework ### 9.2 Risks | Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation | |------|------------|--------|------------| | Distributed transactions (sagas) complexity | High | High | Use saga pattern with compensating transactions; document failure modes | | Inter-service latency accumulation | Medium | Medium | Set circuit breakers with 50ms timeout; cascade timeout budgets | | Kafka consumer lag during spikes | Medium | Medium | Monitor lag metrics; auto-scale consumers; buffer topics | | Data consistency across services | High | High | Eventual consistency acceptable for non-financial data; Ledger is source of truth for balances | | Tokenization Service becomes bottleneck | Low | High | Scale horizontally; dedicated PCI-DSS network segment | | PgBouncer connection exhaustion | Medium | Medium | Monitor connection utilization; tune pool sizes per service | | Risk Service ML model latency | Medium | Medium | Cache ML model in memory; async scoring for non-critical paths | ### 9.3 PCI-DSS Compliance Boundaries **In Scope (CDE):** - Tokenization Service (raw PAN storage and processing) - Risk Service (processes card data for fraud detection) - Network communication between Tokenization Service and card networks **Out of Scope:** - All other services (operate on tokens only) - Client-side SDKs (encrypt card data before transmission) - Ledger Service (operates on monetary values, not card data) **Key Controls:** - AES-256 encryption at rest for token_vault schema - TLS 1.3 for all inter-service communication - No raw PAN logged anywhere except Tokenization Service - Network segmentation via Kubernetes NetworkPolicy - Separate PCI-DSS VPC for Tokenization Service ### 9.4 Circuit Breaker Configuration For circuit breaker patterns (reference: `src/api/circuit_breaker.ts` pattern): | Service | Failure Threshold | Timeout | Half-Open Requests | |---------|------------------|---------|-------------------| | Payment → Card Network | 50% error rate | 30s | 5 | | Payment → Risk Service | 30% error rate | 10s | 3 | | Any → Tokenization | 20% error rate | 5s | 2 | --- ## 10. Helm Chart Structure (Deployment Manifests) ```yaml # values.yaml (representative structure) global: imageRegistry: 123456789.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com imagePullSecrets: ecr-secret apiGateway: replicaCount: 3 resources: requests: cpu: 4 memory: 8Gi limits: cpu: 8 memory: 16Gi hpa: enabled: true minReplicas: 3 maxReplicas: 10 targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 70 paymentService: replicaCount: 3 resources: requests: cpu: 8 memory: 16Gi limits: cpu: 16 memory: 32Gi env: DB_HOST: "{{ .Values.postgresql.payment.host }}" KAFKA_BROKERS: "{{ .Values.kafka.brokers }}" CIRCUIT_BREAKER_THRESHOLD: "50" CIRCUIT_BREAKER_TIMEOUT: "30000" hpa: enabled: true minReplicas: 3 maxReplicas: 20 targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 70 tokenizationService: replicaCount: 2 resources: requests: cpu: 4 memory: 8Gi limits: cpu: 8 memory: 16Gi networkPolicy: enabled: true # Only payment-service can call tokenization allowedNamespaces: - payment-service hpa: enabled: true minReplicas: 2 maxReplicas: 6 postgresql: payment: host: payment-db.cluster-xxx.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com name: payments poolSize: 200 refund: host: refund-db.cluster-xxx.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com name: refunds poolSize: 100 # ... per schema kafka: brokers: - kafka-1.us-east-1.amazonaws.com:9092 - kafka-2.us-east-1.amazonaws.com:9092 - kafka-3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com:9092 topics: paymentEvents: partitions: 32 replicationFactor: 3 retentionHours: 168 ``` --- ## 11. OpenAPI Field-Level Specifications (Complete) The OpenAPI schemas in Section 6 include all request/response fields. Key field specifications for builders: ### Payment Request (Client-Facing) | Field | Type | Required | Validation | Example | |-------|------|----------|------------|---------| | amount | integer | Yes | min: 50 (cents) | 4999 | | currency | string | Yes | 3-char ISO 4217 | "USD" | | paymentMethodId | string | Yes | Token format (pm_*) | "pm_a1b2c3d4" | | idempotencyKey | string | Yes | max 255 chars | "cust_123_20260408" | | metadata | object | No | max 50 keys | {"order_id": "123"} | ### Payment Response | Field | Type | Always Present | Notes | |-------|------|----------------|-------| | id | uuid | Yes | Server-generated | | status | enum | Yes | pending/authorized/captured/voided/failed | | amount | integer | Yes | | | currency | string | Yes | | | paymentMethodId | string | Yes | Token (not PAN) | | createdAt | datetime | Yes | ISO 8601 | | updatedAt | datetime | Yes | ISO 8601 | ### Subscription Request | Field | Type | Required | Validation | |-------|------|----------|------------| | customerId | uuid | Yes | | | planId | string | Yes | | | paymentMethodId | string | Yes | Token (not PAN) | | trialDays | integer | No | 0-90, default 0 | | idempotencyKey | string | Yes | | --- ## Self-Validation Log **Claims classified as fact:** - Service decomposition pattern (hybrid sync/async) - based on publicly documented Stripe/Adyen/Square architectures - PCI-DSS scope boundary (Tokenization Service) - standard industry practice - HPA scaling targets -
